item.title: Metal-and Additive-Free Transfer Hydrogenation of Nitrones to Hydroxylamine Derivatives with PhSiH3
item.description: Hydroxylamine derivatives are essential structural motifs in numerous pharmaceuticals, bioactive compounds, and synthetic intermediates. Despite significant advances, the reduction of nitrones remains challenging owing to their inherent 1,3-dipolar ...

item.title: Rapid, energy-lean sonochemical synthesis of metal-free polybenzoxazine bifunctional photocatalyst for H2O2 production and furfuryl alcohol oxidation
item.description: The development of scalable, selective, and durable photocatalysts remains a major challenge in artificial photosynthesis. Herein, we introduce a new class of metal-free polybenzoxazine frameworks synthesized through an unconventional template- and ...

item.title: Breaking Enantiopurity Paradigm: A Chiral Catalyst That Requires Enantioimpurity for Enantioselectivity
item.description: Asymmetric catalysis traditionally relies on enantiopure ligands to achieve high enantioselectivity. However, nonlinear effects (NLEs) can enable scalemic catalysts to match or even surpass their enantiopure counterparts. Here, we report an unprecedented ...

item.title: Linking wind-power fluctuation to degradation of PEM water electrolyzer
item.description: Proton exchange membrane (PEM) water electrolysis is highly attractive for renewable-powered hydrogen production, yet its degradation under realistic wind-power fluctuations remains insufficiently understood. In this study, a durability assessment ...

item.title: Ageing mechanisms and regeneration of an LMFP/NMC blended cathode
item.description: Ageing and recycling studies are essential to assess the durability and circularity of emerging commercial battery chemistries. Here, the ageing mechanisms of a commercial lithium-ion cells containing an LMFP/NMC blended cathode are investigated after ...

item.title: Smolyak Hierarchy-Inspired Protocol (SHIP): Hierarchical Exploration of Sparse-Reward Spaces with GFlowNets
item.description: Generative modelling is a useful methodology for large-scale combinatorial problems involving many possible candidates. Such problems typically suffer from exponential scaling in complexity as the size of the system being studied is increased, and this ...

item.title: Phosphoestamer Sequence Controls Capture/Release
item.description: While the synthesis of sequence-defined polymers is becoming established, the next frontier is sequence-determined function. Sequenced-defined oligo/polyphosphoesters (phosphoestamers) of dodecane diol (C12) and hexa(ethylene glycol) (HEG) show sequence-...

item.title: Harnessing phosphine oxides interactions with HF and HBF4 to enable the hydrofluorination of alkynes
item.description: Synthetic methodologies for the metal-free fluorofunctionalisation of alkynes to give hydrofluorination products still remain scarce. In this paper, we report on detailed studies on the interaction between the phosphine oxides R3P=O (R = Cy, Ph, Morph) ...

item.title: Towards unified catalyst discovery through transferable composition–activity relationships
item.description: Multinary materials provide vast compositional flexibility for optimizing electrocatalytic reactions, yet systematic exploration of their composition space is challenging. Density functional theory (DFT)-derived models, thin-film materials libraries for ...

item.title: Expanding Metal Complex Enzyme Inhibitor Discovery Through High-Throughput Virtual Screening
item.description: Transition metal complexes occupy regions of three-dimensional chemical space that organic scaffolds cannot easily reach, yet their use in target-based drug discovery has been held back by the absence of structurally diverse, readily available screening ...

item.title: Post mortem insights into swollen LiFePO4 batteries: reversible degradation and suitability for direct recycling
item.description: Lithium iron phosphate (LFP) batteries are increasingly deployed in electric vehicles, raising concerns regarding the management of prematurely retired cells, particularly those affected by swelling. In this work, a comprehensive post mortem investigation ...

item.title: One pot synthesis of carbon dots derived from d-glucose monohydrate: Comprehensive investigation on optical properties and luminescent mechanism
item.description: The best optical properties of N-CDs from D-glucose monohydrate and urea have been synthesized. The use of D-glucose monohydrate as a carbon source and urea as a nitrogen source offers many benefits, including low cost, easy availability, high quantum ...

item.title: Photoluminescent and photoconductive BaZrS3 films grown at device-compatible temperatures from stable sulfide precursors
item.description: Chalcogenide perovskites (CPs) are attractive earth-abundant, non-toxic light absorbers. BaZrS3 is a prominent candidate in the CP family due to its 1.7-2.0 eV band gap, which aligns well with a top absorber in Si-based tandem solar cells. The main ...

item.title: Ultrasensitive Polymeric Fluorescent Nanothermometers Based on Environment-Sensitive DCM-Like Dyes: Decoupling Aggregation, Polarity, and Viscosity Contributions
item.description: Temperature measurements at the nanoscale are essential for understanding physiological and pathological processes. Here, we report fluorescent polymeric nanothermometers (FPTSs) based on thermoresponsive poly(N-isopropylacrylamide) (PNIPAM) ...

item.title: Assembly-by-Elimination of 3D Nanoarchitectures with Designed Interstitials and Interweaves
item.description: The realization of metamaterials with tailored functional responses requires three-dimensional (3D) architectures that combine low packing fractions with complex topology. Such metastructures can enhance strength-to-weight ratios, accommodate large ...

item.title: Tuning hydrogel mechanics through synergistic metal–ligand coordination and chemical fueling
item.description: Chemically fueled anhydride formation offers a powerful strategy to create polymer networks with transient mechanical reinforcement. In this work, metallo-supramolecular hydrogels were developed with two independent crosslinking mechanisms, metal-ligand ...

item.title: Programming Rotational Register to Prescribe Crystal Polymorphism
item.description: Crystal polymorphism underlies many of the distinct properties of condensed matter, yet for atoms and molecules, polymorphs are typically selected by thermodynamics and kinetics rather than by design. Programming crystal polymorphism is particularly ...

item.title: Experimental and Simulation Study of Foliar Nanofertilizers through Cellulose-nanofibril Templating
item.description: Despite the higher absorption efficiency of foliar fertilizers compared to soil fertilizers, the risk of fertilizer loss remains. Additionally, traditional foliar fertilizers tend to form large, irregular particles on leaves, hindering plant absorption. ...

item.title: Mechanistic Determinants of Passive Permeation in Binary Lipid Membranes from Molecular Dynamics Simulations
item.description: Passive permeation is a fundamental determinant of pharmacokinetics. The models that simulate permeation pathways and predict their energetic requirements need to account for the complexity of biological membranes. However, the impact of lipid bilayer ...

item.title: Fragment-based discovery of mycobacterial thioredoxin reductase inhibitors guided by virtual chemical space exploration
item.description: The rising number of people dying from tuberculosis and the increase of drug-resistant Mycobacterium tuberculosis (Mtb) strains lead to an urgent need for new antituberculosis drugs. The mycobacterial thioredoxin reductase (Mtb-TrxR) is a novel and ...

item.title: Beyond Global Drug-Likeness: The FamDLike Framework as a Target Family-specific Druglikeness Score
item.description: Drug-likeness is a central metric which is used to evaluate the physicochemical profile of small molecules either as a filter for virtual screening or, more recently, as a reward function in generative design tasks. Established scores such as ED are ...

item.title: 3D Printing of Metal–Organic Polyhedra: From Porous Molecules to Spatially Programmed Porous Architectures
item.description: Additive manufacturing of microporous materials remains challenging due to the intrinsic processing limitations of extended porous frameworks, which often require binders or complex inks. Herein, we report the first 3D printing of metal–organic polyhedra (...

item.title: Foldamers with Two Distinct Helical Motifs Controlled by Solvent
item.description: Abiotic foldamers that switch between distinct, well-defined geometries are rare. Here, we report a structurally simple series of oligomers with alternatingortho-phenylene and 2,3-pyrazinylene repeat units. Deconvolution of variable-temperature ¹H NMR ...

item.title: Heptacoordinated Antimony(V) Cations
item.description: Heptacoordinated pnictogen cations are quite rare, although cationic Sb(V) species are formally isoelectronic with neutral Sn(IV) compounds, for which several heptacoordinated structures are known. Here, we report on the synthesis, isolation, and ...

item.title: Semicrystalline Chain-growth Photopolymer Networks Synthesized by Orthogonal Neat Polymerization Reactions
item.description: A sequential and orthogonal polymerization reaction strategy was developed to synthesize semicrystalline polymer networks. Acrylate-terminated oligomers were first prepared by thiol-Michael oligomerization between difunctional aliphatic acrylate and thiol ...

item.title: PDB Manipulation Suite (PDBMS): A browser-based toolkit for PDB file manipulation, molecule building and biophysical analysis, easily extendable via reasoning models
item.description: We present here the PDB Manipulation Suite (PDBMS,https://pdbms.altervista.org/), a modular hub of web apps and platforms designed for the seamless creation, edition, and analysis of biomolecular coordinate files, all online and client-side i.e. without ...

item.title: Native multi-dimensional (SEC/CZE/SID) protein complex identification and topology characterization (Native MudPIT)
item.description: The initiation and progression of diseases are usually due to biological processes going awry, driven by proteins in complexes that often assemble via noncovalent interactions of monomeric or multimeric subunits. A broader view of protein complex dynamics ...

item.title: An Alternative Strategy for Albumin Binding in Therapeutics. Proof-of-Concept Synthesis of Metallacarborane–Insulin Conjugates.
item.description: Fear of strict dosing schedules and associated hypoglycaemic events often leads to significant delays in the initiation of insulin therapy in diabetic patients who do not achieve glycaemic targets with oral drugs. To better address these concerns new long-...

item.title: Highly stable microporous water for energy absorption
item.description: Water has poor compressibility. However, introducing hydrophobic porosity into water can make it highly compressible, as water molecules can be squeezed into the hydrophobic pores under pressure. This offers an exciting opportunity to use water to absorb ...

item.title: Non-Significant Effect of Water Content and Precursor Concentration on the Synthesis of Monodisperse SiO2@FexOy Colloidal Spheres
item.description: Monodisperse spherical colloidal particles with core–shell structures, such as SiO₂@shell, are the focus of extensive research. However, those coated with iron oxide (SiO2@FexOy) have received comparatively little attention, rendering them a promising ...

item.title: Water transport in MOF monoliths under pressure
item.description: Pressurised intrusion into hydrophobic metal-organic frameworks (MOFs) is of considerable interest for energy absorption and fluid transport applications, yet the transport mechanisms in monolithic architectures remain poorly understood. Here, in-situ ...

item.title: Electron Activated Dissociation Technique for Middle-Down Mass Spectrometric Analysis of Disulphide Bonds and Free Thiols: Application in Characterising Monoclonal Antibody Biotherapeutic Fragments
item.description: Disulphide bonds contribute to the conformational structure of monoclonal antibodies (mAbs) and are therefore vital for their stability, biological function and safety. However, the biopharmaceutical manufacturing process can influence these bonds, ...

item.description: We demonstrate the utility of back-transformed bootstrap embedding (BE) one- and two-particle density matrices (1 and 2-PDMs) by computing dipole moments and force gradients for several polar systems. The resulting mean absolute error in atomic force ...

item.title: Establishing Quantitative Electrolyte-Solid Electrolyte Interphase Relationships in Lithium Metal Batteries
item.description: Despite the high energy density of lithium metal batteries (LMBs), lithium loss driven by electrolyte decomposition at the highly reductive lithium metal anode to form the solid electrolyte interphase (SEI) remains a critical challenge. Recent studies ...

item.title: In Operando Electrochemical Reduction of Indium Tin Oxide Contacts in Silicon Oxide Memristive Devices
item.description: We report a study of current transient behaviour in silicon oxide memristive devices with indium tin oxide (ITO) top contacts. Constant-current electrical stress reveals that ITO-contacted devices exhibit chaotic conductance fluctuations and a single-...

item.title: Case Study of Bayesian Batch Optimization Strategies for Heterogeneous Catalysts in CO2 Hydrogenation
item.description: High-throughput and automated experimentation platforms combined with artificial intelligence are increasingly used to accelerate catalyst discovery. Batch Bayesian optimization (BO) (i.e., the simulta neous selection of multiple candidate experiments per ...

item.title: Synthesis and Biological Evaluation of O6C-Salvinorin Alkyne Probes
item.description: The salvinorins represent a unique class of polyoxygenated, non-nitrogenous diterpenes that potently and selectively modulate the kappa-opioid receptor (KOR). We recently disclosed a de novo asymmetric synthesis of this scaffold that enabled the discovery ...

item.title: Openclatura–an Open-Source Nomenclature Framework for Rule-Based Molecule Naming
item.description: Naming molecules systematically remains an open challenge in cheminformatics. The reverse task—converting names to structure—has been largely solved for over a decade by OPSIN, yet no open-source tool performs the forward direction. Here we introduce ...

item.title: Effect of washing on the surface chemistry of silica nanoparticles
item.description: The surface chemistry of silica nanoparticles is central to their use in applications such as catalysis, CO2 adsorption, environmental remediation, or drug delivery, yet the role of post synthesis washing remains poorly understood. The surface of silica ...

item.title: Force Fields and Simulation Engines: Lessons Learned from Porting Amber ff14SB and ff19SB to GROMACS
item.description: The GROMACS molecular dynamics simulation package has historically supported a number of molecular mechanics force fields, including Amber. Force fields have evolved over time and continue to do so through refinement of existing interactions and the ...

item.title: Electrocatalytic Hydrodeoxygenation of Aldehydes Enabled by Pd@CNTstabilized Pickering Emulsions
item.description: Electrocatalytic hydrodeoxygenation (ECHDO) has the potential to produce high-value chemicals from aromatic aldehydes using water as a hydrogen source. However, the efficiency of this approach is limited by the low solubility of aromatic aldehydes in ...

item.title: An Autonomous Passive MOF Water Harvester for Extreme Arabian Desert Conditions

item.title: Reactive Molecular Dynamics of Hydrogen Evolution at Charged Interfaces Using Machine Learning Potentials
item.description: Understanding electrochemical reaction mechanisms requires atomistic simulations that simultaneously capture charged interfaces, explicit solvent, and long-time dynamics, which remain beyond the reach of conventional ab initio molecular dynamics. Here, we ...

item.title: Effects of laser beam irradiation on displacement of microparticles at an air–wet gel interface
item.description: Optical trapping based on radiation pressure has enabled advanced manipulation of microparticles. Previous studies have primarily focused on Gaussian-beam optical trapping of nanoparticles using tightly focused laser beams. In such systems, high-intensity ...

item.title: Transition-State Analysis Reveals a Noncanonical Multi-Step Pathway for O-GlcNAc Transferase Substrate Recognition

item.description: Endocrine disrupting chemicals (EDCs) pose complex, multi-mechanistic health risks that are difficult to evaluate using conventional in vitro or in vivo approaches alone. Here, we developed a quantitative adverse outcome pathway (qAOP) model integrating ...

item.title: Phonon Spectra Reveal How Selective Fluorination Enhances Hole Transport in Benzodithiophenes (BDT)
item.description: Selective fluorination is one of the most widely used chemical modifications for improving hole mobility in donor-type organic semiconductors. It is commonly assumed that the benefits arise from isolated geometric or electronic descriptors, such as a ...

item.title: Metal-Organic Cages to functionalize CNTs: higher dispersibility and phase-transport
item.description: Multi-walled carbon nanotubes (MWCNTs) have potential for a range of industrial applications. However, their utility is constrained by challenges related to processing and dispersibility due to their tendency to aggregate in solvents, especially polar ...

item.title: Coherent Pulsed Dynamic Nuclear Polarization with Endogenous Metal-Ions
item.description: Dynamic nuclear polarization (DNP) can overcome the intrinsically low sensitivity of nuclear magnetic resonance (NMR) and enables structural characterization of a wide range of materials. In inorganic solids, DNP can be achieved by introducing ...

item.title: Creatine as an Organocatalyst for the Synthesis of 2,3-Dihydroquinazolin-4(1H)-one Derivatives via Multi-component Reactions at Room Temperature
item.description: Creatine's potential as an organocatalyst for organic synthesis is demonstrated specifically for the preparation of 2,3-dihydroquinazolin-4(1H)-one derivatives. Use of a multi-component sequence involving aldehydes, isatoic anhydride, and ammonium acetate ...

item.title: California’s plan to decarbonize electricity omits key greenhouse gas emissions
item.description: Regional decarbonization or climate action plans in the US generally ignore the greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions of renewable electricity generation systems that occur beyond the operational stage. Our analysis of California’s prospective electricity sector ...

item.title: A Cross-Species Comparison of Excitation Energy Transfer in Red-Lineage Photosystem I
item.description: The Photosystem I supercomplex (PSI) is a central component of photosynthesis in plants and algae. Its antenna subunits absorb the light energy and transfer it through an interconnected pigment network to the reaction center (RC), where it is stored as ...

item.title: The Human ADME Score: A Quantitative Metric of Drug-likeness for Orally Administered Compounds
item.description: Quantitative assessment of ADME-related drug-likeness remains challenging as small-molecule drug discovery expands into larger and more complex chemical space. We hypothesized that drug-likeness for orally administered compounds is best quantified at the ...

item.title: A physical aid to site-selective modification of a peptide: targeting of single residues for conjugation, stapling, and labeling with a nanopore and mechanical positioning
item.description: A physical device to aid post-synthetic modification of specific single residues in a peptide at the single molecule level is proposed; it is similar to a structure that was recently proposed for peptide synthesis without side-chain protection (doi: ...

item.description: We present NovoExpert-2, a dual-method approach to molecular property prediction that achieves state-of-the-art performance on five endpoints of the Therapeutics Data Commons (TDC) ADMET benchmark. Our primary model combines MapLight-style fingerprint fea-...

item.title: A Density Matrix Theory for Photothermal Two-Dimensional Infrared Spectroscopy
item.description: Two-dimensional infrared (2D-IR) spectroscopy can resolve vibrational anharmonicity, mode coupling, and energy transfer. The conventional 2D-IR detects nonlinear polarization that generates propagating photons after sample’s light matter interaction with ...

item.title: ReactiveLJ: A Coordination-Dependent Lennard-Jones-Based Potential for Reversible Bonding in Coarse-Grained Simulations
item.description: Associative polymer networks are cross-linked by reversible bonds whose continual formation, dissociation, and exchange rearrange network topology and thereby govern viscoelastic and transport properties. Coarse-grained (CG) molecular simulations offer a ...

item.title: Photoactivated chlorine dioxide oxygenation of freshly cleaved HOPG surfaces

item.title: Mind the Driver: Behavior-aware Battery Usage Prediction to Reduce Electric Vehicle Range Anxiety
item.description: Range anxiety remains a major concern for electric vehicle (EV) drivers due to unpredictable charge usage influenced by terrain and user behavior variations. To address this issue, we propose a data-driven approach to provide accurate trip-specific ...

item.title: Multiplexed Imaging through Affinity Hierarchies in a Supramolecular Host
item.description: Bioorthogonal chemistry has transformed molecular imaging by enabling highly selective synthetic labeling of biomolecules in their native habitats. However, multiplexed imaging remains fundamentally constrained by the limited number of mutually orthogonal ...

item.title: Lifetime- and potential-engineered photocatalysts for uphill photoredox catalysis
item.description: Most molecular photocatalysts (PCs) have lifetimes ranging from a few nanoseconds to the low microsecond range suitable for photoinduced electron transfer reactions with driving forces larger than –0.2 eV. Very long excited state lifetimes of the PC can ...

item.title: Sb-Doped SrSnO3: a lead free perovskite oxide for sustainable photoresponsive optoelectronics

item.title: Solution-state Magnetic Bistability in Cobalt Corrole Complexes with Switchable Sidearms

item.title: In-context learning meets small molecule property prediction: benchmarking novel machine learning approaches
item.description: Recently, a new category of machine learning approaches for tabular data has emerged: tabular foundation models (TFM), based on in-context learning. A TFM is a neural network (usually a transformer) pre-trained primarily on synthetic data. Its input is an ...

item.title: Novel Theoretical Framework for Coupled Transport in Polymer Melt Extrusion
item.description: Coupled transport phenomena in polymer extrusion have been extensively investigated using analytical, constitutive, and numerical models. While these approaches provide detailed descriptions of individual physical mechanisms, a unified theoretical ...

item.title: Cation competition and low pH protect protocell membranes from divalent cation disruption
item.description: Divalent cations such as Mg2+and Ca2+are essential for many modern biochemical processes and likely played similarly important roles during the origin of life. However, these ions are highly disruptive to fatty acid vesicles, creating a fundamental ...

item.title: L→S2+←L Complexes (Sulfurones) with Unique Architecture: Synthesis, Crystal Structure and Quantum Chemical Analysis

item.description: Agentic research systems have split along a line. Systems that automate the research lifecycle, from ideation to manuscript, now reach flagship venues, but their experiments are machine-learning runs, biomedical hypotheses, or self-written software, not ...

item.title: Quality over quantity in fluorinated battery interphases
item.description: Fluorination of the solid-electrolyte interphase (SEI) is central to achieving high cycling efficiency in lithium metal batteries. However, current approaches often rely on large amounts of fluorinated salts, solvents, or diluents, increasing electrolyte ...

item.title: Reinforcement of Polyvinyl Chloride Using Plasma-Activated Aramid Fibers and Surface-Bonded Compatibilizer
item.description: This study investigated the application of a two-step compatibilization procedure to aramid fiber–poly(vinyl chloride) (PVC) composites. Aramid fibers were subjected to high radio frequency power air plasma treatment and incorporated into the PVC matrix ...

item.title: Self-centring structural damping with MOF-based compressible liquids
item.description: Introducing hydrophobic cavities to water makes a compressible liquid that can absorb mechanical energy. Herein, we present a self-centring, energy-absorbing damping technology based on this material concept and demonstrate its potential in seismic ...

item.title: The Oil Well as the Mine of the Future: Towards In-situ Coproduction of Critical Minerals and Hydrocarbons
item.description: The global demand for critical minerals and materials (CMM) is rapidly outpacing conventional supply chains. This Perspective presents a paradigm shift by demonstrating that mature U.S. sedimentary basins are not merely depleting fuel reservoirs but are ...

item.title: Saturated Organic Poly-ionomer Films Match Proteins as Surprising Electronic Conductors
item.description: We demonstrate efficient long-range electronic transport through ultrathin (<100 nm) multilayers of highly charged, non-conjugated polyionomers (PIs), a class of organic materials fundamentally distinct from conventional organic semiconductors. Layer-by-...

item.title: A Computational Study of Gemcitabine Encapsulation in CC3 Porous Organic Cage: Molecular Docking, Molecular Dynamics, Binding Free Energy, and Structure-Activity Relationship Analysis
item.description: Gemcitabine, a first-line nucleoside analogue chemotherapy drug, is rapidly inactivated in plasma by cytidine deaminase (CDA), severely limiting its therapeutic efficacy. Porous organic cages (POCs) have emerged as promising drug delivery vehicles due to ...

item.title: Light-Driven Control of Polar Order in Ferroelectric Nematic Liquid Crystals
item.description: Ferroelectric nematic (NF) liquid crystals are polar fluids that combine long-range orientational order with spontaneous macroscopic polarization and exceptionally large dielectric permittivity. While these properties make NF materials attractive for ...

item.title: Integrated GEMC–MD Simulation Study of Phase Behavior and Interfacial Structure in the Benzene–Methanol Azeotrope
item.description: Azeotropes, mixtures that exhibit constant boiling points and behave as single substances, pose significant challenges in separation processes and are crucial in various industrial applications such as chemical manufacturing and purification. In this ...

item.title: Collision Induced Unfolding Predicts the Aggregation of Glycoengineered Monoclonal Antibodies
item.description: Monoclonal antibody (mAb) therapeutics exhibit substantial structural heterogeneity, requiring analytical methods capable of rapidly assessing critical quality attributes (CQAs) that influence stability and developability. Non-native aggregation is a key ...

item.title: MolecuAR: A Multilingual WebAR Molecular Visualization Tool Connected to PubChem for Chemical Education
item.description: Three-dimensional molecular representation is important in chemistry education, yet preparing interactive augmented-reality resources can require specialized authoring and molecule-specific assets. MolecuAR is an open, mobile-first WebAR educational ...

item.title: CatGo: Bridging CLI Coding Agents with Interactive Structure and Workflow Management for Computational Chemistry
item.description: Computational chemistry is increasingly limited not by individual calculations but by the manual effort of assembling, parameterizing, executing, and monitoring multi-step workflows across heterogeneous engines and computing environments; existing ...

item.title: SurfKit: Multi-property surfactant prediction combining experimental data and molecular dynamics simulations

item.title: Spin–Phonon Coupling in Molecular Qubits and Organosuperelasticity in Molecular Crystals: A Review
item.description: Molecular symmetry, rooted in group theory, provides a unifying language that connects the spatial arrangement of atoms to the electronic, magnetic, and mechanical behaviour of matter. This review examines two arenas in which symmetry-based reasoning has ...

item.title: Integrating GC-MS with Computational Toxicology for Pesticide Risk Assessment
item.description: Pesticide risk assessment separates analytical detection from toxicological evaluation, leaving a practical gap: regulatory-compliant residue levels reveal nothing about the biological hazard of detected compounds, and computational toxicology models are ...

item.title: Ion Mobility-Selected Collision Induced Unfolding Differentiates Biosimilars Based on Glycan Content
item.description: As patents expire for monoclonal antibody (mAb) therapeutics, biosimilar development has emerged as a critical strategy for improving therapeutic accessibility. One critical quality attribute, glycosylation, can alter the efficacy, stability, and half-...

item.title: Digital Analytical Twins: A Specimen-Centric Architecture for Self-Driving Analytical Laboratories
item.description: Self-driving laboratories increasingly combine robotic handling, automated instruments, workflow orchestration, machine learning, digital twins and autonomous experiment planners. In this literature the central question is usually framed as: which ...

item.title: Ion Mobility-Collision Induced Unfolding Detects Stability Shifts within Hidden Protein Conformer Families
item.description: Proteins commonly exist in complex mixtures, adopting a diverse ensemble of structural conformations. While some of these conformations are functionally important, others (e.g. pre-aggregative states), can adversely affect protein function. In monoclonal ...

item.title: A Unified Total Synthesis of Eudesmane-Type Sesquiterpenoid Dimers Chlorahupetenes A, B and E.
item.description: Chlorahupetenes A (1) and B (2) are eudesmane-type sesquiterpenoid dimers that contain unprecedented, rearranged carbon skeletons that were isolated as enantiomeric pairs by Liu and coworkers in 2022. Herein, we disclose the first total synthesis of ...

item.title: A Synthesis of a Fluorinated Tetrapeptide Substrate, (4-Fluoro-F)LQS, to Monitor SARS-CoV-2 Main Protease Activity (Nonstructural Protein 5, NSP5) Supports Cleavage between Leucine and Glutamine
item.description: Severe acute respiratory coronavirus-2 (SARS-CoV-2) caused the global pandemic in 2020. The inhibition of NSP5 of SARS-CoV-2, the main cysteine protease, is a strategy to kill the virus. This paper describes in more detail the eSorts that were involved in ...

item.title: Compensated ferrimagnetism above 600 K in a metal-organic framework
item.description: Compensated ferrimagnets combine long-range magnetic order with small stray magnetic fields, but realizing such behavior over broad temperature ranges in chemically designable materials remains challenging. Recently, radical-bridged pyrazine frameworks ...

item.title: Capturing Activity Trends in Metal-Organic Framework Catalysis using Minimal Active Site Models
item.description: Metal-organic frameworks (MOFs) offer immense potential as catalysts owing to their modular and tunable structures, yet their vast combinatorial design space makes discovery challenging. Although realistic models of MOF active sites are effective for ...

item.title: High-throughput multireference calculations of magnetic properties of lanthanide complexes
item.description: Molecular magnets based on lanthanides are promising candidates for quantum information technologies. However, progress toward practical applications is limited by the small number of compounds with useful magnetic properties. Computational screening ...

item.title: Operando observation of immobilized carbonic anhydrase for CO2 conversion
item.description: Heterogeneous catalysis with immobilized enzymes is pivotal for continuous and sustainable industrial bioprocesses. However, the relationship between enzyme dispersion on solid supports and catalytic activity is not well described. Here, we investigate ...

item.title: Mechanochemical C‑Terminal Activation of Peptides: Solvent‑Minimal Thiocarboxylation with Suppressed Epimerization
item.description: A solvent‑minimal mechanochemical approach enables one‑step peptide C‑terminal thiocarboxylation with efficient suppression of epimerization. The method affords peptide thiocarboxylic acids from diverse substrates, including epimerization‑prone dipeptides,...

item.title: Nickel-Catalyzed Enantioselective Radical C(sp3)–C(sp3) Cross-Coupling
item.description: Enantioselective radical–radical cross-coupling represents a powerful yet largely unsolved strategy for constructing stereogenic C(sp3) centers, because simultaneously controlling cross-selectivity and absolute stereochemistry between two distinct, ...

item.title: Epimer-Dependent Solid-State Landscapes of Trospium and Exotrospium Halide Salts
item.description: Trospium (TR) and exotrospium (ETR) form a closely related epimeric pair of quaternary ammonium compounds, differing only in the stereochemistry of the tropane ring system. While the solid-state chemistry of trospium has been studied mainly for its ...

item.title: Spatial placement of half-Salen-bound nickel single-atoms on MOFs governs cooperative photocatalytic hydrogen evolution
item.description: The integration of single-atom catalysts into metal–organic frameworks (MOFs) offers a promising strategy for coupling molecular catalytic sites with photoactive architectures. While previous studies have focused primarily on the identity and coordination ...

item.title: Half-edge graphs, Lewis Formulas, and Electron Pushing
item.description: Lewis structures are frequently used to display the electronic structure of molecules. Chemical reaction mechanisms, on the other hand, are commonly visualized by means of “curved arrows” that denote the movement of electron pairs or single unpaired ...

item.title: How the Oxidation State Drives Bonding Properties in CeIII and CeIV Tris(amidinate) Complexes
item.description: We report the synthesis and characterization of a pair of homoleptic cerium tris(amidinate) complexes, [CeIII{PhC(NtBu)2}3] and [CeIV{PhC(NtBu)2}3][Al(OC4F9)4], containing cerium in both its trivalent (CeIII) and tetravalent (CeIV) oxidation states. To ...

item.title: Unlocking the potential of Glycosidase-Cleavable AgroDrug Conjugates for Sustainable Agriculture
item.description: The Crop protection industry is currently locked in a race to discover the next generation of efficacious, safe and disruptive modalities. Towards this endeavour, Agrodrug conjugates (AgDCs) offer many unique opportunities to tune the properties of active ...

item.title: Ex Situ Kinetic and Thermographic Profiling for Solvent-free Air-tolerant Giese-type Addition of Boronic esters onto Electron-Deficient Olefins
item.description: We describe a dual Lewis base mechanophotocatalytic protocol for Giese-type additions that unites an iridium photocatalyst with Lewis bases under solvent-minimized or solvent-free conditions. The combination of mechanical agitation and visible-light ...

item.title: Virtual Screening and In Silico Evaluation of Antidepressants as Novel MexB Efflux Pump Inhibitors in Pseudomonas aeruginosa
item.description: Background: Multidrug-resistant (MDR) Pseudomonas aeruginosa poses a critical therapeutic challenge, primarily mediated by overexpression of the MexAB-OprM efflux pump system. Classical efflux pump inhibitors (EPIs) such as PABÉTAN and NMP show promising ...

item.title: Structure and Stability Prediction of Binary and Ternary Molecular Mixed Crystals Using Ensemble Free Energies
item.description: Mixed crystals provide a route to tune the properties of molecular solids through continuous changes in composition. However, predicting their stability is challenging because it requires determining both substitution energies and configurational entropy ...

item.title: Atomically Defined Photoswitchable Hybrid Nanostructures Built from Functionalized Norbornadienes and Cobalt Oxide Nanoislands on Au(111)
item.description: Organic photoswitches on surfaces enable the construction of interfaces that can store solar energy (molecular solar thermal systems, MOST) or change their functional properties in response to light (photoresponsive interfaces). However, many ...

item.title: A Review on F-Block Elements and Their Role in Nuclear Energy and Power Generation
item.description: This paper intends to shed light on the F-block elements and their critical significance in nuclear energy, from their physical and chemical properties to their conventional uses. This paper covers all the F-block elements, with an emphasis on the details ...

item.title: The Golden Path to Brightness: Unlocking Room-Temperature Rare Earth Luminescence with Phosphinocarboxylate-based RE/Au Complexes
item.description: Rare earth/transition metal (RE/TM) complexes offer unique opportunities to combine complementary electronic properties within a single molecular framework but remain challenging to access because of the distinct coordination preferences of both metal ...

item.title: Fundamentals of Electrochemical Energy Storage: Theory and Practice
item.description: The rechargeable alkali-ion battery has emerged as a transformative technology, revolutionizing portable energy storage in a wide range of applications, from small electronic devices to large electric vehicles. In this review, we combine modern ...

item.title: Wise Co-Solvent Selection for Modulating Bromide Solvation and Improving the Efficiency of Flowless Zn-Br2 Batteries
item.description: Flowless aqueous Zn-Br2 batteries (ZBBs) are attractive for safe, low-cost stationary energy storage, yet remain limited by bromine-related self-discharge and a rate-dependent efficiency trade-off: coulombic efficiency (CE) often increases at high current ...

item.title: Light-Gated Targeting of DNA Replication Foci in Cancer Cells
item.description: DNA three-way junctions (3WJs) are transient non-canonical DNA structures linked to replication stress, genome instability and cancer, but their controlled recognition remains rare and has not yet been demonstrated in cells. Here we report the first light-...

item.title: Chemical Shift Prediction Beyond the Electronic Structure Limit
item.description: Nuclear magnetic resonance (NMR) is a critical tool for developing pharmaceuticals, studying proteins, and identifying natural products. However, many diagnostic chemical shift variations remain too small to be interpreted by computational methods, making ...

item.title: Unlocking the activities of polyglutamylation enzymes in brain tubulin with top-down mass spectrometry
item.description: Microtubules, essential components of the cytoskeleton, are formed from a− and b−tubulin heterodimers which are organized in a cylindrical fashion, like a barrel. The C-terminal tubulin tails remain exposed on the outside of the MT, where they can be ...

item.description: Microplastic (MP) particles undergo simultaneous structural and morphological transformations during weathering. These processes are expected to generate surface features across multiple length scales, ranging from micrometere-sized cracks and fractures ...

item.description: Short peptides are promising building blocks for supramolecular soft materials yet controlling when and where hydrogelation occurs remains challenging. Here, we report a reactionprogrammed strategy for peptide hydrogel formation based on a biphasic ...

item.title: Water transport in MOF monoliths under pressure
item.description: Pressurised intrusion into hydrophobic metal-organic frameworks (MOFs) is of considerable interest for energy absorption and fluid transport applications, yet the transport mechanisms in monolithic architectures remain poorly understood. Here, in-situ ...

item.title: A Stable Ruthenium(CAAC-5) Olefin Metathesis Catalyst for Bioconjugation
item.description: Olefin metathesis is a powerful transformation for carbon–carbon bond formation, yet its application in biological settings is challenged by the instability of most ruthenium catalysts in aqueous media. Herein, we report a stable Ru(CAAC-5) olefin ...

item.title: Catalytic Uncaging of Anticancer Drugs via a Buchner Ring-Expansion

item.title: Scalable and Generalizable Analog Design via Learning Medicinal Chemistry Intuition from Matched Molecular Pair Transformations
